title: compact_block and block_range tests are pure self-oracles
description: compact_block_proto_fields_are_accessible and block_range_construction (client.rs lines 277-310) construct proto structs with literal values and immediately read the same fields back. These tests prove only that the Rust struct fields are accessible by name — they do not validate protobuf serialization or wire compatibility. A test that constructs a struct with field X=V and then asserts field X == V is a self-oracle: it would pass even if prost generated completely wrong serialization. A stronger test would serialize to bytes and deserialize with a reference decoder (e.g., the prost decode path or a known-good hex fixture), or cross-check against a field that exercises a non-trivial code path.
close_reason: P3/P4 deferred: below stopping threshold (P0=0, P1=0, SEC7 says STOP)
